message:
fix(tests,bd-3e3qg.6): flip last snake_case err.kind assertion to kebab-case [f52aec01-followup]

Lib unit test `cli_read_db_tests::active_index_busy_cli_error_is_clean_and_structured`
was the sole remaining `cargo test --lib` failure on this branch
(4032 passed / 1 failed). Commit f52aec01 normalized every runtime
err.kind value to kebab-case ("index-busy") and efe5e8f5 updated most
downstream test assertions — but this one payload assertion at
src/lib.rs:~13679 still expected snake_case "index_busy" and tripped
every run:

    assertion `left == right` failed
      left:  Some("index-busy")
      right: Some("index_busy")

The sibling assertion four lines earlier (on `err.kind` directly) was
already kebab-case; only the derived JSON payload assertion was
stale. Flipped to "index-busy" — no production code change, the
payload field has been kebab-case since f52aec01.

Previously surfaced as a handoff to ProudLake via Agent Mail #288
when their exclusive reservation on src/lib.rs was still active; now
that the reservation has lapsed I took it directly to unblock the
3e3qg.6 test-suite-green-gate pass.

Verified locally:
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/rch_target_cass_p3 \
  rch exec -- cargo test --lib \
    cli_read_db_tests::active_index_busy_cli_error_is_clean_and_structured
  -> 1 passed in 0.00s (was the only --lib failure)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
